specific_mechanism: Thermoacoustic engines convert temperature gradients to acoustic oscillations via standing waves in resonators; phase relationship between pressure and velocity determines efficiency (Rayleigh criterion)
llm_application: Treat LLM training-loss oscillations as thermoacoustic standing waves; gradient noise = acoustic pressure, batch update = velocity; engineer phase-coherent training schedule that maintains the Rayleigh criterion to amplify rather than dissipate useful gradient signal.
novelty_claim: No published 2024-2026 LLM work formalizes thermoacoustics phase-coherence mapping as proposed.
